title: lysimachia crista-galli
intro: Lysimachia crista-galli, also known as golden seal or yellow seal, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive seal-shaped markings on its leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. The medicinal properties of golden seal have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving oral health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
